page: Amazon Web Services t3.2xlarge is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t3 server family. It is built on the Nitro hypervisor and features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. This CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, providing 8 virtual CPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is generally weak, as evidenced by the stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% categories respectively. Multi-core performance is relatively stronger, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores reaching average levels. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks consistently register in the average tier. However, complex processing tasks such as bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing show poor performance, placing in the bottom 10%.

The t3.2xlarge offers a qualitative cost-efficiency profile suited for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Because it utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, it is designed to handle temporary spikes in demand without the cost overhead of continuously allocated compute resources. However, the tradeoff is limited sustained CPU performance, as sustained heavy workloads will deplete CPU credits and trigger performance throttling. This instance is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, development environments, low-to-medium traffic web serving, and background task processing where continuous maximum CPU performance is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.2xlarge is an x86_64 burstable instance within the t3 family, utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 2.5 GHz clock speed, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, and it provides a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core metrics and average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Due to its burstable CPU allocation, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with fluctuating resource demands. However, it presents a tradeoff of throttled performance under sustained high CPU utilization. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, development environments, and moderate web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering average multi-core performance for variable workloads.

tagline: A burstable x86_64 instance with balanced memory and variable CPU performance for non-continuous, spike-prone workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.
Features a burstable CPU allocation model suitable for handling temporary demand spikes.
Delivers average multi-core performance alongside weak single-core benchmark results.
Best suited for general-purpose workloads, development environments, and moderate web serving.
